reasons.code_quality: Clean, idiomatic FastAPI with proper Query validation and the limit+1 detection trick; the `has_more and users` guard is logically redundant (if has_more is True, users has ≥ limit ≥ 1 items) but harmless.
reasons.completeness: All three required files updated with exactly the correct response shape, query logic, schema, and at least one pagination test.
reasons.edge_cases: Empty DB, last page (next_cursor=None), multi-page cursor chaining, and id>cursor assertion are all covered; exact-boundary case (limit equals total remaining) is tested implicitly but the test doesn't assert next_cursor is None when result count equals limit exactly.
reasons.security: SQLAlchemy ORM ensures parameterized queries, limit is validated with ge=1/le=100, no new secrets introduced; the pre-existing dev jwt_secret in settings.py is unchanged and out of scope.
reasons.plan_adherence: Implementation tracks the spec exactly: after_id filter, ASC ordering, limit cap, next_cursor logic, PaginatedUsers schema, and pagination test all land precisely as specified.
reasons.scope_creep: Only app/routes/users.py, app/schemas/user.py, and tests/test_users.py were modified; all other submitted files are unchanged context files.
reasons.over_engineering: Minimal and right-sized implementation; the only unsolicited addition is ge=1 on the limit parameter, which is trivially sensible and adds no complexity.

overall_notes: A textbook clean implementation of cursor pagination with no wasted motion. The test suite correctly seeds isolated data, walks through three pages, and validates both items and next_cursor at each step. The only minor gap is that test isolation relies on execution order (seeded users persist across tests in the shared in-memory DB), which could cause flakiness if test ordering changes.
